maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 5, 2018 at 04:16
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[AUGUSTUS WELBY NORTHMORE PUGIN DRAWINGS ])//EN" "pugin.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

did.unittitle: Augustus Welby Northmore Pugin drawings.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: 63 pencil, pen and ink, and wash drawings, signed, of buildings and architectural details, sculpture, and other subjects, chiefly in France, Northern Germany and Antwerp.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James Mackey in 1995 on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund. Osborn Accession 95.3.3.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The A. W. N. Pugin Drawings is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: A. W. N. Pugin Drawings. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection. Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

bioghist.head: AUGUSTUS WELBY NORTHMORE PUGIN (1812-1852)
bioghist.p: Augustus Welby Northmore Pugin (1812-1852), English architect, designer and author, was a leading figure in the Gothic Revival and the Oxford Movements. His designs include St. Chad's Cathedral, Birmingham, Balliol College, Oxford, and St. Augustine's Church, Ramsgate. His True Principles of Pointed or Christian Architecture (1841) inspired John Ruskin. During his last years he worked with Sir Charles Barry on the new Palace of Westminster. Pugin died in London on September 14, 1852.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Osborn Collection of drawings by Augustus Welby Northmore Pugin (1812-1852) is listed according to the catalog numbers given in the Catalogue of the Drawings Collection of the Royal Institute of British Architects: The Pugin Family, by Alexandra Wedgwood. Brief descriptions are provided in this list: for full details, see the Wedgwood catalogue.

Many of the drawings have been rematted for exhibition use, as a result of which they are stored according to size. The list of drawings is arranged in Wedgwood number order, with the box location of each drawing indicated to the left of each entry.

corpname.part: Koninklijk Museum voor Schone Kunsten (Belgium) ( Pictorial works..)

did.unittitle: Ramsgate, The Grange: View of the Oratory, from the door looking East. Pencil & wash
Ramsgate, The Grange: View of the Passage and Staircase outside the Oratory. Pencil & wash
Antwerp, Royal Museum of Fine Arts: Angel of the Annunciation by Simone Martini. Pen & watercolor
Antwerp, Royal Museum of Fine Arts: Lectern, morse, cup with hinged lid, and priest celebrating Mass before altar... from the triptych of the seven sacraments by Roger van der Weyden. Pen
Antwerp, Royal Museum of Fine Arts: Kneeling, censing angel & banderole from panel of the Benediction formerly attributed to Justus van Gent. Pen
Antwerp, Royal Museum of Fine Arts: The Holy Women at the Tomb by Gerard David, with studies of heads to larger scale. Pen & watercolor
Antwerp, Royal Museum of Fine Arts: Head of Virgin from panel by Quentin Massys, with details of left eye & edging of cloak. Pen
Antwerp, Royal Museum of Fine Arts: Kneeling abbot from right-hand panel of diptych by "Bruges Master of 1499," with details. Watercolor & pen
Antwerp, Royal Museum of Fine Arts: Seated Virgin from Calvary by Antonella da Messina, with details including canopy from panel by Master of Hoogstraeten

Antwerp, Royal Museum of Fine Arts: Standing figure of St. Paul from fresco by Bicci di Lorenzo, flying angel holding cloth canopy from painting of standing Virgin by Jan van Eyck, kneeling nun from panel of Christ before the Crucifixion by follower of Cornelius Engelbrechts, and cusping from portrait of an unknown canon by Memling. Watercolor & pen
Louvain, Church of St. Peter: Studies of crozier and cross from wings of triptych by Dieric Bouts. Pen
Namur, Convent of Les Soeurs de Notre- Dame: General view, studies of a lecturn, studies of a ring and a foot reliquary. Pen
Amiens, Cathedral: View of North transept facade & North side of nave. Pencil
Amiens, Church of St. Germain: View of portal. Pencil
Amiens, Church of St. Leu: View of West front from street, with towers of cathedral in background. Pencil
Amiens: View of facade and tower of late Gothic parish church. Pencil
Beauvais, Cathedral: Exterior of chevet. Pencil
Beauvais, Cathedral: Facade of North transept. Pencil
Beauvais, Cathedral: 14th century Clock in North transept. Pen & pencil
Beauvais: View of street with timber- framed houses looking towards gateway. Pen
Blois: View of medieval street crossed by enclosed wooden bridge. Pen
Bourges, Hotel Cujas: View of courtyard. Pencil
Evreux, Cathedral: Pulpit seen across nave. Watercolor
Evreux, Cathedral: North transept rose window & triforium. Watercolor
Gisors, Church of St. Gervais: View of West front. Pencil
Gisors, Church of St. Gervais: Chevet from Southeast. Pencil
Honfleur: View of shore and ships. Pencil
Noyon, Cathedral: 17th century tomb slab of priest, with details. Pen over pencil
Noyon, Cathedral: 15th century tomb slab of priest, with details. Pen over pencil
Paris, Church of St. Gervais & St. Protais: Interior view of axial chapel, showing altar with high reredos. Pencil
(4 drawings in one mat, numbered 1 through 4) Poitiers, Ducal Palace & Hotel de la Prevote: part-plans, external elevations, view from street, and details of statuary, tracery, moldings, etc. Pen
Rouen, Cathedral: S. transept looking South in West aisle, with kneeling figures. Watercolor
Rouen, Cathedral: North transept East aisle. Watercolor
Rouen, Church of St. Andre-aux-Febres: View of choir & tower from street looking Northwest. Pencil
Rouen, Church of St. Maclou: View of North transept rose window and triforium. Watercolor
Rouen, Church of St. Patrice: View in North aisle. Watercolor
Rouen, Museum of Antiquities: Studies of medieval metalwork. Pen & watercolor
Rouen, Rue des Moulins: View showing tall timber-framed houses. Pencil
Rouen: View of narrow street with tall timber-framed houses. Pencil
Rouen: View of tall timber-framed house on street corner. Pencil
Villeneuve-les-Avignon, Porte St. Andre: View of entrance. Sepia wash
Cologne, Cathedral & Church of St. Ursula: Studies from stained glass windows. Pen & watercolor

Cologne, Cathedral & Church of St. Ursula: Studies of a standing figure of St. Sebastian from a stained glass window in the cathedral, and of a stone statue of St. Ursula in the church of St. Ursula, with part-plans and details of pedestal. Pen, watercolor, & wash
Cologne, Cathedral & Church of St. Ursula: Studies from stained glass windows, reliquary busts, and a metal crucifixion group. Pen & watercolor
Cologne: Views of church of Klein St. Martin and of an unidentified church. Pen
Freiburg-im-Breisgau, Cathedral: Studies of Stained Glass. Pen & watercolor over pencil
Luebeck, Cathedral: Plans, elevations, & details of late Gothic chandelier decorated with seated figures of bishops and angels. Pen
Luebeck, Cathedral: Study of the tomb effigy of Bishop Heinrich von Bockholt, with details of costume and frame. Pen
Nuremberg, Deer park gate: View from Bergstrasse. Sepia wash
Ulm, Cathedral: View of choir stalls. Sepia wash & pencil
View of unidentified church on cliff overlooking the Rhine. Sepia & blue wash
Baslow: View of 3-arched medieval bridge. Pen & pencil
Gedney, Church of St. Mary Magdalen: Studies of 15th century tracery and 14th century porch arch. Pen
Shrewsbury, Church of St. Mary: Studies of flowing tracery and stained glass. Pen
Distant view of tall ruins on a hill with houses in foreground. Watercolor
Basle, Church of St. Martin: View from Southeast, with medieval conduit in foreground. Pencil
Basle: Street scene with late Gothic houses. Pencil
Fribourg: View of street and church with tall needle spire, with church on hilltop in background. Sepia wash
Lucerne: Street scene with old houses and canal. Pencil
Lucerne: View inside Hofbruecke. Pencil
